original: NHL NY Islanders score 3 shorthanded goals against Washington Capitols
Colo is born, the first gorilla to be bred in captivity at Columbus Zoo and Aquarium, Columbus, Ohio
Argentine soccer superstarLionel Messiscores his 644th goal for FC Barcelona during 3-0 win over Real Valladolid to break Pele's record for most goals for one club; Pele, 643 goals for Santos 1956-74

Christmas novelty single "The Chipmunk Song (Christmas Don't Be Late)" by David Seville and the Chipmunks reaches #1 - the only holiday chart-topper until Mariah Carey’s "All I Want for Christmas Is You" in 2019
First flight of the US aircraft Lockheed SR-71, reaches 3,530 kph (record for a jet)
Thailand adopts constitution
Ebola vaccine VSV-EBOV is found to be 70-100% effective in a study published in The Lancet, becoming the world's first proven vaccine against Ebola
Srinivasa Ramanujan, Indian mathematician (number theory), born in Erode, British India (d. 1920)
Yuri Ivanovich Malinchenko, Russian cosmonaut (1st person to marry in space), born in Khrushchev, Ukrainian SSR, USSR
India score 7-676 v Sri Lanka at Kanpur in Cricket

Cold wave: -6°F in Tulsa, Oklahoma; -12°F in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania; -18°F in Denver, Colorado; -23°F in Kansas City, Missouri; -42°F in Scottsbluff, Nebraska; -47°F in Hardin, Montana; and -60°F in Black Hills, South Dakota
Steelers' Kordell Stewart runs quarterback record 80 yds for TD
Beethoven's Symphonies No. 5 and No. 6, Choral Fantasy and Piano Concerto No. 4 (featuring the composer as soloist) premiere at the Theater an der Wien in Vienna, Austria
"The Mummy" directed by Karl Freund and starringBoris Karloff, is released in the US, the first Mummy horror film
Paul Coffeybecomes the second NHL defenceman to record 1000 points, doing so in a record-breaking 770 games
Tsunami hits Indonesia's Sunda Strait killing over 400 after part of the Anak Krakatoa volcano slips into the sea
Joseph Muhler, American biochemist and dentist (developed Crest - 1st fluoride toothpaste), born in Fort Wayne, Indiana (d. 1996)[1]
Archie Andrews, American comic book character created by Bob Montana (ARCHIE!), "born" in Riverdale
Dmitry Bilozerchev, Russian gymnast (Olympic gold USSR x 4 1988; World C'ship gold x4 1983, x4 1987), born in Moscow, Russia
Jules Janssen flies in a balloon in order to study a solar eclipse

In the NHL's first official week of play, 3 players score hat tricks in the same game, for the same team; Corb Denneny, Reg Noble, & Harry Meeking each score 3 for the Toronto Arenas in an 11-4 win over the Ottawa Senators
Australian archaeologist Sue O'Connor finds first evidence of modern humans in Jerimalai cave, near Lene Hara cave in East Timor
Jean-Michel Basquiat, American artist (Gray, SAMO), born in Brooklyn, NYC, New York (d. 1988)
KUAC TV channel 9 in Fairbanks/College, AK (PBS) begins broadcasting
Caitlin Clarkregisters her first triple-double at Iowa with 13 points, 13 rebounds, and 10 assists against Western Illinois
Henry Armstrong, American boxer (world featherweight, lightweight, welterweight [19 x title defences] champion), born in Columbus, Mississippi (d. 1988)
Piero Angela, Italian science journalist and television host (Quark), born in Turin, Italy (d. 2022)
Larry Stevenson [Richard], American skateboard innovator (kicktail), born in Santa Monica, California (d. 2012)
James Burke, British science historian and broadcaster (Connections), born in Derry, Northern Ireland
Great Britain sets national maximum road speed at 70 miles per hour
New Orleans wide receiver Michael Thomas sets NFL record for most catches in a season with his 144th as Saints beat Tennessee 38-28; breaks 17-year old record held by Marvin Harrison (143)
1st string of Christmas tree lights created byThomas Edison
Ballon d'Or: Manchester United striker Denis Law wins award for best European football player; beats Inter midfielderLuis Suárezand Real Madrid winger Amancio Amaro; first Scotsman to win the award
Alexander Gordon Laing, Scottish explorer (1st European to reach Timbuktu), born in Edinburgh, Scotland (d. 1826)
Rick Nielsen, American rock vocalist, guitarist, and songwriter (Cheap Trick - "I Want You to Want Me"; "The Flame"), born in Elmhurst, Illinois
Luna H. Mitani, Japanese-American surrealist painter, born in Hokkaido, Japan
Casper Ruud, Norwegian tennis player (French Open 2022, 23 & US Open 2022 runner-up), born in Oslo, Norway
Army of Moroccan Pasha Ahmad al-Mansur begins its epic 135 day crossing of the Saharan desert on his way to defeat the Songhai Empire
Germans demand surrender of American troops at Bastogne, Belgium
Claude Debussy's first orchestral masterpiece "Prélude à l'apres-midi d'un faune" premieres in Paris
Indiana Pacers win, 133-132 in Denver to end a 28 NBA game losing streak on the road
South Africa signs accord granting independence to South West Africa
Zimbabwe & England draw Bulawayo Test Cricket with England needing 1 run to win
Major winter storm across the US causes widespread disruption, prompting states of emergency to be issued in Wisconsin and New York
Retrospective show of Mexican artistDiego Riveraopens at The Museum of Modern Art in New York
Teresa Carreño, Venezuelan classical pianist, soprano, composer, and conductor, born in Caracas, Venezuela (d. 1917)
Grote Reber, American radio astronomer (1st parabolic radio telescope), born in Wheaton, Illinois (d. 2002)
Barbara Billingsley, American actress (Leave it to Beaver), born in Los Angeles, California (d. 2010)
Lynne Thigpen, American actress (Where in the World Is Carmen Sandiego?; An American Daughter), born in Joliet, Illinois (d. 2003)
Tyrell Biggs, American boxer (Olympic gold super heavyweight 1984; World C'ship gold 1982), born in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania
Jonathan Joss, American comedic actor (King of the Hill, Parks and Recreation), born in San Antonio, Texas (d. 2025)[1]
Basshunter [Jonas Erik Altberg], Swedish record producer and DJ (Now You're Gone), born in Halmstad, Sweden
Jordin Sparks, American singer and American Idol winner, born in Phoenix, Arizona
Josef Newgarden, American auto racer (Indianapolis 500 2023, 24; IndyCar Series 2017, 19; Indy Lights 2011), born in Nashville, Tennessee
Latto [Alyssa Michelle Stephens], American rapper (Big Energy), born in Columbus, Ohio
Joshua Bassett, American actor and singer (High School Musical: The Musical: The Series), born in Oceanside, California
Moravian missionary constructs 1st schoolhouse west of Allegheny

Dutch people revolt against meat tax
